channel.title: The Rest of the Record — All Sources

item.title: DOHMH - BFSCS v. 0462026F0: Thermometer for measuring the temperature of food not provided to measure temperatures of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods (TCS) during cooking, coolin
item.description: Outcome: Default/ No Appearance

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) NAQ FY26
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) providers through a variety of program models, serves youth ages 14-24 throughout New York City by providing opportunities to become familiar with the world of work, gain employment experience, and identify educational pathways that support career and life goals. This program is integral to the City s workforce development strategy and provides a critical intervention point for underserved youth population.

item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/DPR/NEW
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials
Notice of Intent to Issue New Solicitation(s) Not Included in FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the mayor will be issuing the following solicitation(s) not included in the FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule that is published pursuant to New York City Charter § 312(a):?? Agency: DPR-Expense Description of Services to be provided: LiDAR Based Street Tree Inventory for New York City Anticipated Contract Start Date: 1/1/2027 Anticipated Contract End Date: 12/31/2027 Anticipated Procurement Method: RFP Job Titles: None Headcounts: 0

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) NAQ
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) providers through a variety of program models, serves youth ages 14-24 throughout New York City by providing opportunities to become familiar with the world of work, gain employment experience, and identify educational pathways that support career and life goals. This program is integral to the City s workforce development strategy and provides a critical intervention point for underserved youth population.

item.title: Environmental Protection — Procurement: 82625B0023-BCS-SM005 Staten Island, Small Meter & MTU
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Furnishing all labor and equipment with specified DEP-supplied material to repair, replace or install new water meters and meter transmission units in the borough of Staten Island to support small meter replacement program. Small meter replacement/installation.

item.title: Housing Preservation and Development — Procurement: Sole Source Notice of Intent- Routesmart FY27 -29
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Pursuant to Section 3-05(C) of the City's Procurement Policy Board Rules, The New York City Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) intends to enter into a Sole Source negotiation for Routesmart Software which is a critical service for inspectors in HPD, who perform the Housing Quality Standards (HQS) inspections. All routine HQS inspections, as well as the ones that are routed and created by 311 complaints for Section 8 units, use the Routesmart software. Routesmart Software TM is a proprietary product of RouteSmart Technologies Inc. This Sole Source procurement is being mana

item.title: Design and Construction — Public Comment on Contract Awards: HWCD101G - Construction Materials Testing for Independent Assurance.
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that the Department of Design and Construction is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type: Contract Contractor: CM Testing Lab Inc Contractor Address: 31 Denton Avenue, Garden City Park, NY 11040-4046 Scope of Services: HWCD101G - Construction Materials Testing for Independent Assurance. Maximum Value: $250,000.00 Term: 1095 Calendar Count Days E-PIN: 85026W0011001 Procurement Method: MWBE Small Purchase Procurement Policy Board Rule: Section 3-08 (c)(1)(iv) How can I comment on this proposed contract award? Please submit your comment

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Public Comment on Contract Awards: Supporting Father Involvement Subscription
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that The Department of Youth & Community Development (DYCD) is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type: Contract Contractor: Children s Hospital Corporation Contractor Address: 300 Longwood Avenue, Boston, MA 02115 Scope of Services: The Department of Youth and Community Development (DYCD) will subscribe to Supporting Father Involvement ( SFI ) courses to support DYCD Fatherhood Initiative programs. The SFI program courses, also known internationally as Parents as Partners (PasP), is an evidence-based approach to increasing father invo

item.title: Small Business Services — Public Comment on Contract Awards: FY26 Laptop Purchase
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that NYC Small Business Services is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type: MWBE Non-Competitive Small Purchase Contractor: Derive Technologies LLC Contractor Address: 40 Wall Street, 20 th Floor, New York, NY 10005 Scope of Services: Laptop Purchase for Agency Maximum Value: $108,859.00 Term: 7/1/25 through 6/30/26 Renewal Clauses : None E-PIN: 80126W0011001 Procurement Method: Small Purchase. Procurement Policy Board Rule: 3-08 (c)(1)(iii) How can I comment on this proposed contract award? Please submit your comment to procurementhe

item.title: Education — Procurement: New Best Value Request. Requirements Contract for Medical Waste Removal Services and Supplies
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
The New York City Department of Education ( DOE ) requests on behalf of the Division of the Office of Occupational Safety and Health to release a Best Value Request for Bid (RFB) seek qualifies and licensed vendors to provide comprehensive medical waste collection, transportation, and disposal services across school facilities citywide, services will support school-based health centers, and nursing offices. Detailed service descriptions and requirements are provided in the Request for Bid (RFB) solicitation. BIDS MUST BE RECEIVED BY NO LATER THAN 4:00 P.M. EST, Thursday, July 9, 2026. LATE BID

item.title: Transportation — Procurement: In-Process Fabrication Inspection of Structural Steel in the United States and Canada
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
This Procurement is subject to participation goals for Minority-Owned Business Enterprises (MBEs) as required by Section 6-129 of the New York Administrative Code. The MWBE goal for this project is 30%. A Pre-Proposal Conference (Optional) has been scheduled for May 26, 2026, Time: 10:00 AM through Zoom. Proposers who wish to attend the Zoom Conference meeting can access by using the Zoom Link provided in the 'Prepare RFX' Section - SETUP Tab - Pre-Proposal/Pre-Bid Conference Section in PASSPort. This Request for Proposals (RFP) is released through PASSPort, New York City s online procurement

item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/DOT/NEW
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials
Notice of Intent to Issue New Solicitation(s) Not Included in FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the mayor will be issuing the following solicitation(s) not included in the FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule that is published pursuant to New York City Charter § 312(a):? Agency: Department of Transportation Description of Services to be Provided: REI services in connection with the marine when & where structural repairs and other miscellaneous work directed within the five boroughs Anticipated New Start Date: 12/1/2026 Anticipated New End Date: 2/

item.title: Board Meetings — Public Hearings and Meetings: BOARD MEETINGS
item.description:
Outcome: Public Hearings and Meetings
BOARD MEETINGS ¦ NOTICE OF MEETINGS City Planning Commission Meets in NYC City Planning Commission Hearing Room, Lower Concourse, 120 Broadway, New York, NY 10271, twice monthly on Wednesday, at 10:00 A.M., unless otherwise ordered by the Commission. City Council Meets by Charter twice a month in Councilman s Chamber, City Hall, Manhattan, NY 10007, at 1:30 P.M. Contract Awards Public Hearing Meets bi-weekly, on Thursday, at 10:00 A.M. In order to access the Public Hearing and testify, please call 1-646-992-2010, Access Code: 715 951 139, no later than 9:55 A.M. Civilian Complaint Review Boa

Outcome: Procurement
Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) providers through a variety of program models, serves youth ages 14-24 throughout New York City by providing opportunities to become familiar with the world of work, gain employment experience, and identify educational pathways that support career and life goals. This program is integral to the City s workforce development strategy and provides a critical intervention point for underserved youth population.

item.description: Outcome: Agency Rules

item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Upgrade Scooter Software's Beyond Compare Enterprise Edition to v5

item.title: Parks and Recreation — Procurement: Maintenance and Operation of the Alley Pond Environmental Center
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
The City of New York Department of Parks and Recreation ( Parks ) has awarded a concession to Alley Pond Environmental Center, Inc. ( Licensee ), of 229-10 Northern Boulevard, Douglaston, NY 11362, for the maintenance and operation of the Alley Pond Environmental Center in Douglaston, Queens. The concession was awarded through a Different Procedure (Sole Source) pursuant to Section 1-16 of the Concession Rules of the City of New York. All gross receipts received by Licensee will be used exclusively to provide for the maintenance, operation, and programming of the Licensed Premises. Fees will b

item.title: Economic Development Corporation — Procurement: Consultant Services, Outside Counsel Litigation RFP
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
New York City Economic Development Corporation ( NYCEDC ) is seeking one or more law firms to serve as outside litigation counsel to NYCEDC in connection with certain pre-litigation and litigation matters related to lease enforcement, property management and other landlord-tenant and commercial litigation issues. This representation may include insurance, bankruptcy and other business and real estate issues as well as collection work. The successful Respondents will investigate pre-litigation matters, draft litigation-related documents including summonses, complaints, affidavits, briefs, court

item.title: Information Technology and Telecommunications — Procurement: Participatory Budget Operations (CEC)
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
FY26 OPS Contract_20250510470

item.title: Buildings — Procurement: Investigative Services - NYCHA Boiler Explosion
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
Emergency contract for NYCHA Boiler Explosion to provide licensed forensic engineers to perform full-time field investigative services. Photographing and documenting structural and foundation elements, preparing structural and foundation plans, reviewing historic records, collecting and processing data, and analyzing mechanical components.

item.title: Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration — Public Comment on Contract Awards: EMERGENCY RESPONSE SYSTEMS

item.description:

This Procurement is subject to participation goals for Minority-Owned Business Enterprises (MBEs) as required by Section 6-129 of the New York Administrative Code. item.title: Community Boards — Public Hearings and Meetings: May 20, 2026 PUBLIC HEARING
item.description:
Outcome: Public Hearings and Meetings
NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the following matters have been scheduled for Public Hearing by Community Board: BOROUGH OF BROOKLYN COMMUNITY BOARD NO. 18 Wednesday, May 20, 2026, 7:00 P.M., 1097 Bergen Avenue, Board Office Meeting Room and via Zoom for participants who wish to participate online. The NYC Department of Small Business Services will deliver a brief presentation on NYC BEST, highlighting programs, resources, and services designed to support and strengthen small businesses across New York City. This Statutory Public Hearing has been duly advertised in the City Record. Please Note:

item.title: Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration — Procurement: IT Consulting Services for CurRent NYC Project
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement
The vendor will provide consulting services for the Current NYC. The period of performance will be for thirty-six (36) months from May 1,2026 to April 30,2029. CurRent NYC The business objectives of the CurRent NYC initiative involve an application that would provide all landlords doing business with HRA with improved customer service, including self-service, facilitate electronic payment of rent, and make payments to landlords accurately across all programs and tenants (the golden record concept). The system in production facilitates CityFHEPS application submission, processing, and eligibi

The ONS MAP Mediation program provides borough-based conflict mediation services as an alternative to court involvement, enabling parties to volu

item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/DHS/EXT
item.description:

item.title: Citywide Administrative Services — Public Comment on Contract Awards: 85626W0060001 Elevator Dual Plunger Retrofit Brakes
item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards
This is a notice that NYC Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS) is seeking comments from public about the proposed contract below. Contract Type : General Contract (CT1) Contractor: New York Elevator Contractor Address: 81 Central Hwy, Suite 52, Stony Point, NY 10980 Scope of Services : To furnish, deliver, and provide dual plunger retrofit brake kids ('Goods') and technical support for legacy traction elevators with single-plunger brakes at various Department of Citywide Administrative Services ('DCAS') facilities located throughout the five (5) boroughs of the City of New Yor
